learning_outcomes.description: Identify value judgements and ethical judgements inherent in economic theory and policy.
Examine how ethical thinking and ethical decision-making can be applied in economic and social context.
Apply ethical frameworks to decision-making in economic, social, and political contexts to address real world problems.
Critically reflect on ethical responsibilities of economists in academic and policy debates.
Communicate and defend ethical arguments in addressing economic and social problems.

assessments.due_date:
Workshop reflections start in Week 2 and end in Week 11

Reflective Disputation 1: 18/09/2026 3:00 pm

Reflective Disputation 2: 23/10/2026 3:00 pm
assessments.task_description:
This task consists of two parts

Workshop Reflections (10%)

Students will be required to complete 5 of 8 workshop session reflections, worth 10% in total. These shorter reflections are designed to help you develop your reflection skills and strengthen your critical thinking. They also provide a structured way to generate and refine ideas that will support your two longer reflective disputations.

Reflective Disputations (30%)

Students will also be required to write two reflective disputations. Each disputation will involve reflecting on a presentation delivered by other students. Each reflective disputation is worth 15%.

The insights and ideas developed through your workshop reflections should be directly incorporated into these disputations. In particular, you should draw on the patterns of thinking, critique, and synthesis that you practice in the shorter reflections to produce more developed and cohesive arguments in the longer reflective disputations.

Further details will be provided on Blackboard

Your online reflective disputations cannot be on the same topic as your presentation or policy brief.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students must attend the workshop for access to the workshop reflections. Submitted online. See Blackboard for detail.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: During Allocated Week (determined in week 1-2 workshop). The class schedule will be made available as a link on the BB website.
assessments.task_description:
Students are required to complete a group presentation during the semester. Working in groups, students are required to present a persuasive perspective on an ethical issue related to economics and public policy. These issues will be on the topics covered in weeks 4 to 12. Students will elect a group and a topic in Week 1/2. Groups are of 3 - 4 students. The topics aligned with the weekly learning topics and a list will be made available in the first workshop (week 1) and sign-up for topics will occur over weeks 1 - 2. Any student who does not sign up by Week 2 will be allocated a group and topic at the start of week 3. Students are required to submit their topic for consideration by Week 3 as part of this assessment.

  • Students must present in the week in which they sign up - no exceptions.
  • You must be ready to present at your scheduled time. Late starts will not be accommodated.
  • As this represents the secure assessment for this course - students cannot have any electronic device on them during the presentation (this includes mobile phones and iPads).
  • ANY STUDENT FOUND IN POSSESSION OF NOTES DURING THIS ASSESSMENT WILL BE CONSIDERED IN BREACH OF ACADEMIC INTEGRITY POLICIES. ALL NOTES WILL BE CONFISCATED AND THE MATTER WILL BE REFERRED AS A CASE OF ACADEMIC MISCONDUCT.
  • Presentations will be completed using only the Lecture Room Computer. No personal computers or devices can be used to drive the PowerPoint. Students should bring a USB device to upload their presentation to the University Computer.
  • Students must show their ID to teaching staff for verification before presenting.
  • Presentations will be recorded on the lecture recordings.

Presentations are limited to 12 - 15 minutes per group. You will be asked questions in relation to the material and views presented. Each student must present for a minimum of 3 minutes as part of the assessment task. Q&A also counts to your mark. You will have a maximum of 1 minute to respond to the question. Each student must answer a question related to the topic.

Slides must be submitted 24 hours prior to your presentation online to TurnItIn to check for plagiarism. No more than 12 slides can be presented, with no more than 600 words total (excluding a slide with references). 

Your presentation topic cannot be on the same topic as your online reflective disputations or policy brief.

Further details will be provided on Blackboard.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Presentations will be completed in the workshop times scheduled.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
As part of the policy brief, students are expected to conduct through research on the selected policy issue, analyse the economic aspects of the policy issue and apply an ethical lens to evaluate the policy dilemma. A list of policy topics will be provided.

There is an expectation that a student will complete sufficient research on the selected topic, including historical context and current challenges. Data should be collected to support analysis and arguments. Economic analysis is required as well as the application of ethical frameworks to the issue selected.

- The text component of the brief is limited to 2 pages.

- Font required is 12pt Times New Roman. 

- Normal margins required - (2.54cm top, bottom and sides)

Please See Blackboard for specific question, details, and criteria. Submit via Turnitin via Blackboard.

Your policy brief cannot be on the same topic as your presentation or online reflective disputations.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
